message:
test(core): property-based tests for memory budget invariants (wa-5477)

20 proptest properties covering BudgetLevel ordering/display/serde,
MemoryBudgetConfig serde/defaults, PaneBudget usage_ratio/thresholds,
BudgetSummary serde/counts, and MemoryBudgetManager register/unregister.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-0je","title":"[EPIC] Phase 1 Testing: Unit tests, integration tests, E2E with detailed logging","description":"# Phase 1 Testing Epic

## Purpose
Comprehensive testing infrastructure for Phase 1 components. Every component MUST have thorough tests before Phase 1 can be considered complete.

## Testing Philosophy
- **Unit tests**: Test each module in isolation with mocks
- **Integration tests**: Test component interactions
- **E2E test scripts**: Shell scripts that test real behavior with detailed logging
- **Golden corpus**: Regression fixtures for pattern matching
- **Property-based tests**: Use proptest for invariant verification

## Components to Test

### 1. Workspace & Crate Scaffolding (wa-4vx.1)
- [ ] Cargo workspace compiles successfully
- [ ] All lints pass (clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings)
- [ ] Formatting verified (cargo fmt --check)
- [ ] No unsafe code (compile-time forbid)

### 2. WezTerm Interface Layer (wa-4vx.2)
- [ ] Unit tests for CLI command builders
- [ ] Mock tests for response parsing (list_panes, get_text)
- [ ] Error handling tests (pane not found, socket errors)
- [ ] Integration tests with fixture JSON responses
- [ ] E2E script: `scripts/test_wezterm_interface.sh`

### 3. Storage & Indexing (wa-4vx.3)
- [ ] Schema migration tests
- [ ] Unit tests for StorageHandle operations
- [ ] FTS5 search correctness tests
- [ ] Segment sequence monotonicity tests
- [ ] Gap detection and recording tests
- [ ] Concurrent read/write tests (WAL mode verification)
- [ ] Property-based tests: inserted text is searchable

### 4. Ingest Pipeline (wa-4vx.4)
- [ ] Delta extraction correctness tests
- [ ] Overlap matching algorithm tests
- [ ] Adaptive polling behavior tests
- [ ] Backpressure and bounded channel tests
- [ ] Gap event emission tests
- [ ] Property-based: segments in sequence

### 5. Pattern Detection Engine (wa-4vx.5)
- [ ] Quick reject performance tests
- [ ] Individual pattern rule tests (positive + negative)
- [ ] Golden corpus regression fixtures:
  - tests/corpus/codex/*.txt + *.expect.json
  - tests/corpus/claude_code/*.txt + *.expect.json
  - tests/corpus/gemini/*.txt + *.expect.json
- [ ] Extraction correctness tests (named captures)
- [ ] Performance budgets: p50 < 1ms, p99 < 5ms

## E2E Test Script Requirements
All E2E scripts must:
1. Print timestamped log messages
2. Report PASS/FAIL for each test case
3. Save detailed logs to /tmp/wa_e2e_*.log
4. Exit 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure
5. Support --verbose flag for extra detail

## Directory Structure
```
tests/
├── unit/
│   ├── wezterm_client_tests.rs
│   ├── storage_tests.rs
│   ├── ingest_tests.rs
│   └── pattern_tests.rs
├── integration/
│   ├── storage_integration.rs
│   └── pipeline_integration.rs
├── corpus/
│   ├── codex/
│   ├── claude_code/
│   └── gemini/
scripts/
├── e2e_test.sh (master script)
├── test_wezterm_interface.sh
├── test_storage.sh
├── test_ingest.sh
└── test_patterns.sh
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All unit tests pass: `cargo test --all-targets`
- All E2E scripts pass: `./scripts/e2e_test.sh`
- Test coverage > 70% for core modules
- No regressions in golden corpus
- Performance budgets met

 {"id":"wa-0mx7","title":"WeztermInterface trait: abstraction layer for real and mock implementations","description":"# WeztermInterface trait

## Purpose
Create an abstraction layer over WezTerm interactions that allows swapping between real CLI and mock implementations.

## Current State
wa-core directly calls WezTerm CLI commands. This needs to be abstracted.

## Interface Design
```rust
#[async_trait]
pub trait WeztermInterface: Send + Sync {
    /// List all panes across all windows
    async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>>;
    
    /// Get text from a pane's scrollback
    async fn get_text(&self, pane_id: u64, options: &GetTextOptions) -> Result<String>;
    
    /// Send text to a pane (keystrokes)
    async fn send_text(&self, pane_id: u64, text: &str) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Spawn a new pane
    async fn spawn(&self, options: &SpawnOptions) -> Result<PaneInfo>;
    
    /// Split a pane
    async fn split(&self, pane_id: u64, direction: SplitDirection) -> Result<PaneInfo>;
    
    /// Activate (focus) a pane
    async fn activate(&self, pane_id: u64) -> Result<()>;
    
    /// Get pane info by ID
    async fn get_pane(&self, pane_id: u64) -> Result<Option<PaneInfo>>;
}

pub struct GetTextOptions {
    pub start_line: Option<i64>,
    pub end_line: Option<i64>,
    pub escapes: bool,
}

pub struct SpawnOptions {
    pub domain: Option<String>,
    pub cwd: Option<PathBuf>,
    pub command: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

pub enum SplitDirection {
    Horizontal,
    Vertical,
}
```

## Real Implementation
```rust
pub struct WeztermCli {
    socket_path: Option<PathBuf>,
    timeout: Duration,
}

#[async_trait]
impl WeztermInterface for WeztermCli {
    async fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>> {
        let output = Command::new("wezterm")
            .args(["cli", "list", "--format", "json"])
            .output()
            .await?;
        // Parse JSON...
    }
    // ... other methods
}
```

## Factory Pattern
```rust
pub fn create_wezterm(config: &Config) -> Box<dyn WeztermInterface> {
    if config.simulate {
        Box::new(MockWezterm::new())
    } else {
        Box::new(WeztermCli::new(&config.wezterm))
    }
}
```

## Migration Path
1. Define trait
2. Implement for existing WeztermCli
3. Update callers to use trait
4. Implement MockWezterm (separate task)

## Testing
- Trait method contract tests
- Real implementation tests (with WezTerm)
- Factory tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WeztermInterface trait covers all operations
- [ ] WeztermCli implements trait
- [ ] All existing code migrated to use trait
- [ ] Factory creates appropriate implementation
- [ ] Tests verify trait contract","notes":"Migrated remaining WeztermClient call sites in wa-core/wa to WeztermHandle (default_wezterm_handle) + WeztermHandleSource for waits. WorkflowRunner/WorkflowContext now use PolicyGatedInjector<WeztermHandle> alias; tests updated. WeztermInterface gains circuit_status(). cargo fmt/check/clippy/test all pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:52:49.54500413Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.217143-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:22:29.525376256Z"}

 {"id":"wa-0z88","title":"Docs: incident bundles + wa reproduce (shareable support workflow)","description":"# Task: Document incident bundles and `wa reproduce`

## Goal
Make incident bundles discoverable and usable by humans and by support processes.

  - what data is included by default (and what is not)

- Include examples of:
  - a policy denial bundle
  - a rule replay bundle
  - a watcher crash bundle

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new contributor can follow docs to generate and replay a bundle without guesswork.

 {"id":"wa-4ym","title":"[EPIC] Observability Infrastructure: Structured Logging, Metrics, Health Endpoints","description":"# [EPIC] Observability Infrastructure

## Mission
Make wa **fully observable** with structured logging, metrics export, and health endpoints that enable debugging, monitoring, and alerting.

## Why This Matters
wa runs as a background daemon managing critical automation. When things go wrong:
- Logs must be **structured** (JSON) for parsing by log aggregators
- Metrics must be **exportable** for dashboards and alerts
- Health endpoints must be **queryable** for orchestration

Without observability, debugging is guesswork.

## Components

### 1. Structured Logging Infrastructure
All log output follows a consistent structured format:
```rust
#[derive(Serialize)]
struct LogEntry {
    timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,
    level: Level,
    target: String,
    message: String,
    // Contextual fields
    pane_id: Option<u32>,
    workflow_id: Option<String>,
    event_id: Option<i64>,
    // Performance
    duration_ms: Option<f64>,
    // Error context
    error: Option<ErrorContext>,
}
```

Features:
- JSON output for log aggregators (ELK, Loki, etc.)
- Human-readable output for terminals (default)
- Log levels: trace, debug, info, warn, error
- Contextual fields propagated via tracing spans
- Sensitive data redaction (tokens, passwords)

### 2. Metrics Export
Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint:
```
# Counters
wa_events_detected_total{type="usage_limit",agent="codex"} 42
wa_workflows_completed_total{workflow="handle_usage_limits",status="success"} 38
wa_policy_decisions_total{decision="allow",action="send"} 1024

# Gauges  
wa_panes_observed 4
wa_panes_ignored 1
wa_queue_depth 0
wa_db_size_bytes 1234567

# Histograms
wa_pattern_match_duration_seconds_bucket{le="0.001"} 9500
wa_workflow_step_duration_seconds_bucket{le="1.0"} 450
```

### 3. Health Endpoint
HTTP endpoint for orchestration health checks:
```bash
$ curl localhost:9876/health
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "checks": {
    "wezterm_connection": "ok",
    "database": "ok",
    "pattern_engine": "ok"
  },
  "uptime_seconds": 3600,
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

Liveness vs Readiness:
- `/health/live` - process is running
- `/health/ready` - ready to handle requests

### 4. Tracing Integration
Distributed tracing via OpenTelemetry:
- Trace IDs propagated through workflows
- Spans for key operations
- Export to Jaeger/Zipkin (optional)

## Configuration
```toml
[observability]
log_format = "json"  # or "pretty"
log_level = "info"
metrics_enabled = true
metrics_port = 9876
health_port = 9876
tracing_enabled = false
tracing_endpoint = "http://localhost:4317"
```

## Testing
- Unit tests: Log formatting, metrics collection
- Integration tests: Health endpoint responds correctly
- E2E tests: Metrics increment as expected during workflows

## Success Criteria
- All log output is structured and parseable
- Metrics endpoint serves Prometheus-compatible data
- Health endpoint enables orchestration integration
- Sensitive data never appears in logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Structured logging produces JSON with required fields and redaction applied.
- Metrics endpoint exports Prometheus-compatible counters/gauges/histograms.
- Health endpoints (/health, /health/live, /health/ready) return correct status codes and JSON.
- Observability configuration is documented and validated via tests in `wa-086`.
- No secrets appear in logs or health payloads.

 {"id":"wa-55y","title":"wa robot workflow abort: safely cancel running workflows","description":"# wa robot workflow abort

## Purpose
Allow agents to safely abort running workflows when they're stuck, no longer needed, or conditions have changed.

## Command Interface
```bash
# Abort by execution ID
wa robot workflow abort <execution_id>

# Abort with reason (recorded in audit)
wa robot workflow abort <execution_id> --reason "Manual intervention required"

# Force abort (skip cleanup steps)
wa robot workflow abort <execution_id> --force
```

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "command": "workflow.abort",
  "data": {
    "execution_id": "wf-abc123",
    "aborted": true,
    "workflow_name": "handle_usage_limits",
    "pane_id": 3,
    "previous_status": "running",
    "aborted_at_step": 3,
    "step_name": "browser_auth",
    "reason": "Manual intervention required",
    "forced": false,
    "cleanup_performed": true,
    "aborted_at": 1737205500000
  }
}
```

## Abort Behavior
1. **Normal abort** (default):
   - Mark workflow as aborting
   - Run any cleanup steps defined by workflow
   - Release pane lock
   - Record in audit trail

2. **Force abort** (--force):
   - Immediately terminate
   - Skip cleanup steps
   - Release pane lock
   - Record force-abort in audit

## Error Cases (Stable Codes)
- E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND: No workflow execution with given ID
- E_ALREADY_COMPLETED: Workflow already finished
- E_ALREADY_ABORTED: Workflow already aborted
- E_ALREADY_FAILED: Workflow already failed
- E_ABORT_FAILED: Cleanup step failed (logged, abort continues)

## Safety Considerations
- Abort should not leave pane in bad state
- If mid-send, let current send complete
- If waiting for pattern, cancel the wait
- Release pane lock so other workflows can run

## Audit Trail
```json
{
  "action": "workflow_abort",
  "execution_id": "wf-abc123",
  "reason": "Manual intervention required",
  "forced": false,
  "requester": "robot-mode",
  "timestamp": 1737205500000
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_abort_running_workflow() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    
    assert!(output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(output["data"]["aborted"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["data"]["previous_status"], "running");
}

#[test]
fn test_abort_with_reason() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id, "--reason", "Test abort"]);
    
    assert!(output["data"]["aborted"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["data"]["reason"], "Test abort");
}

#[test]
fn test_abort_force_skips_cleanup() {
    let exec_id = start_workflow_with_cleanup();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id, "--force"]);
    
    assert!(output["data"]["aborted"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(output["data"]["forced"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert!(!output["data"]["cleanup_performed"].as_bool().unwrap());
}

#[test]
fn test_abort_normal_runs_cleanup() {
    let exec_id = start_workflow_with_cleanup();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    
    assert!(output["data"]["cleanup_performed"].as_bool().unwrap());
}

#[test]
fn test_abort_not_found_error() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", "nonexistent-id"]);
    
    assert!(!output["ok"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND");
}

#[test]
fn test_abort_already_completed() {
    let exec_id = run_workflow_to_completion();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    
    assert!(!output["data"]["aborted"].as_bool().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(output["data"]["error_reason"], "already_completed");
}

#[test]
fn test_abort_releases_pane_lock() {
    let exec_id = start_workflow_with_pane_lock(3);
    execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    
    // Verify pane is no longer locked
    assert!(!is_pane_locked(3));
}

#[test]
fn test_abort_json_schema_validation() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-abort.json");
}
```

### E2E Test
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_abort.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="${ARTIFACT_DIR:-/tmp}/workflow_abort.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Workflow Abort E2E ==="

# 1. Start a workflow (if we can)
log "Attempting to start workflow..."
RUN=$(wa robot workflow run handle_compaction 0 2>&1 || true)
log "Run result: $RUN"

EXEC_ID=$(echo "$RUN" | jq -r '.data.execution_id // empty')
if [ -z "$EXEC_ID" ]; then
    log "No execution ID, testing error case..."
    ABORT=$(wa robot workflow abort "fake-exec-id" 2>&1 || true)
    log "Abort fake: $ABORT"
    echo "$ABORT" | jq -e '.error.code == "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND"' || { log "FAIL: wrong error"; exit 1; }
    log "Got expected not-found error"
else
    # 2. Abort the running workflow
    log "Aborting workflow $EXEC_ID..."
    ABORT=$(wa robot workflow abort "$EXEC_ID" --reason "E2E test abort")
    log "Abort result: $ABORT"
    echo "$ABORT" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: abort failed"; exit 1; }
    echo "$ABORT" | jq -e '.data.aborted == true' || { log "FAIL: not aborted"; exit 1; }
    
    # 3. Verify workflow is now aborted
    log "Verifying status..."
    STATUS=$(wa robot workflow status "$EXEC_ID")
    log "Status after abort: $STATUS"
    echo "$STATUS" | jq -e '.data.status == "aborted"' || { log "FAIL: status not aborted"; exit 1; }
    
    # 4. Verify double-abort returns correct error
    log "Testing double-abort..."
    DOUBLE=$(wa robot workflow abort "$EXEC_ID" 2>&1 || true)
    log "Double abort: $DOUBLE"
    echo "$DOUBLE" | jq -e '.data.error_reason == "already_aborted"' || { log "WARN: double abort handling"; }
fi

log "=== PASS: workflow_abort ==="
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Abort stops running workflow safely
- [ ] Cleanup steps run unless --force
- [ ] --force flag skips cleanup
- [ ] --reason flag records reason in audit
- [ ] Pane lock released after abort
- [ ] Audit trail records abort with reason
- [ ] JSON validates against wa-robot-workflow-abort.json schema
- [ ] Error cases return stable E_* codes
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes with detailed logging

 {"id":"wa-56pt","title":"Extend browser smoke-test/doctor checks to Anthropic+Google","description":"# Task: Extend browser smoke-test/doctor checks to Anthropic + Google

## Goal
Make it easy to validate browser automation health per service/account without running a full workflow.

## Design
Extend the existing browser smoke-test command (Phase 2) so it supports:
- `--service anthropic`
- `--service google`
- optional `--account <key>`

Command behavior:
- Non-destructive: only opens pages and checks auth state.
- Clear exit codes and JSON output in robot mode.
- Human mode uses rich output (Phase 4), but v0.1 can start with plain text.

## Outcome taxonomy (must align with auth realities matrix)
Use the same canonical outcomes as `wa-nu4.2.3.5`:
- `Automated`
- `NeedsHuman`
- `Fail`

If the CLI chooses user-friendly labels (e.g., Success/NeedsHuman/Failed), it **must** also emit the canonical outcome field for parity.

## What to test
- Profile directory exists/created.
- Playwright initialization works.
- Service landing page reachable.
- Auth state detection works:
  - signed in → `Automated`
  - signed out/MFA → `NeedsHuman`
  - selector drift or navigation failure → `Fail`

## Deliverables
- CLI entrypoint (or `wa doctor --browser`) that runs these checks.
- Structured error mapping so workflows can call the same code paths.
- Outcome mapping consistent with the auth realities matrix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running smoke-test for each service produces one of:
  - `Automated` (already logged in)
  - `NeedsHuman` (with next steps)
  - `Fail` (with actionable message)
- Outcomes are consistent with the matrix (no ad-hoc state names).


## Testing
- Manual test matrix:
  - Run per service with an already-authenticated profile and verify `Automated`.
  - Run with a fresh profile and verify `NeedsHuman` (not `Fail`).

- Artifact/redaction checks:
  - Ensure any captured screenshots/logs redact or avoid tokenized URLs.

 {"id":"wa-ruus","title":"Track 1: Basics - What is wa? Start watching. View status (5 min)","description":"# Track 1: Basics

## Purpose
Introduce wa to new users in under 5 minutes. They should understand what wa does, successfully start it, and see meaningful output.

## Target Audience
Users who just installed wa and want to understand what it does.

## Exercise Sequence

### Exercise 1.1: What is wa?
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Brief explanation with ASCII diagram
- **Duration**: 30 seconds
- **Goal**: User understands wa is a "terminal hypervisor for AI agents"

### Exercise 1.2: Check WezTerm
- **Type**: Verification
- **Requires**: WezTerm running
- **Action**: User runs `wa doctor` (or tutorial auto-checks)
- **Success**: "✓ WezTerm detected with 2 panes"
- **Fallback**: Simulation mode shows mock output

### Exercise 1.3: Start Watching
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Requires**: WezTerm running
- **Action**: User runs `wa watch` (tutorial may auto-start)
- **Success**: "wa is now watching 2 panes"
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 1.4: View Status
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: User runs `wa status`
- **Success**: Tutorial explains each column
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 1.5: Your First Event
- **Type**: Simulated (unless agent present)
- **Action**: Tutorial injects mock compaction event
- **Success**: User runs `wa events` and sees it
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Track Completion
- Achievement unlocked: "First Watch"
- Prompt to continue to Track 2

## Testing
- Golden output tests for each exercise explanation
- Integration test for full track in sandbox mode
- E2E test with real WezTerm (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5 exercises implemented
- [ ] Each exercise has clear instructions and success criteria
- [ ] Sandbox mode works for all exercises
- [ ] Track completion triggers achievement
- [ ] Total time <5 minutes in testing","notes":"Implemented Basics track as 5 exercises in crates/wa-core/src/learn.rs (what-is-wa, doctor check, start watcher, view status, first event). Enabled simulation mode for all basics exercises. Updated achievement mapping so first_watch unlocks on basics.3 and first_event on basics.5/events.1. Added regression assertions for 5 exercises + all-simulatable basics. Validation: cargo test -p wa-core learn; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:13.680730807Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.204171-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:25:08.046374553Z"}
